summary: Next.js provides a layered error handling architecture through file conventions (error.tsx, not-found.tsx, global-error.tsx) and special functions (notFound, redirect). Proper use of these features ensures resilient applications with good user experience during failures.
reasoning: error.tsx files create automatic React Error Boundaries scoped to route segments, isolating failures and preventing full-page crashes
Server Components can throw errors that are caught by the nearest error.tsx boundary, but layout errors require a parent boundary
redirect() and notFound() use Next.js's internal throw mechanism, so wrapping them in try/catch will break their functionality
Structured error responses from Server Actions allow Client Components to display contextual error messages and maintain form state
Input validation at the server boundary prevents injection attacks, data corruption, and provides early, clear error feedback
